So let me give you a little bit of a challenge. Like what would you, let's say someone needs to come up with ideas to sell a financial newsletter. It's a financial newsletter about dividends, which has been of course, you know, a boring thing and endlessly written about, right? Your task with come up with a new exciting way, it's gonna get people that's gonna sell this newsletter about dividends. So how do you, what would you do? How would you go about getting GPT to assist you with that? Getting AI to assist? Yeah, I would first put together any information I have about the avatar, whoever it's supposed to be buying this thing, right? And just anyone as an answer is not a good answer for any, and you know this, but for marketing copyright, you can't just do anyone who is into dividends should buy this as an awful way to sell anything. So put together, I don't know, five to six bullet points that describe who the avatar is, like what they hope to get, what they hope to achieve, what they're struggling with, and just generically who they are, you just need like five to six bullet points, just baseline understanding. And I just want to interrupt you for a second, because it's something that I wanted to say when you were talking about the other thing, feeding in stuff and asking it to do a profile, you can get AI to help you profile your avatar by giving it people that have reviewed books on Amazon, giving it copy that other, your competitors have done, giving it copy that you've done, giving it other write ups, giving it letters that you've gotten from prospects. And it can tell you who your avatar is. Exactly right. So you either know already who the avatar is, or AI can help you formulate who your avatar is based on text you give it. So either way, you have five to seven bullet points, let's just say whether you come out with it, or AI helps you come out with it, you got those bullet points. And then you have another summary bullet points about, like in this case, the dividend newsletter, like what it is, how it works, and what kind of recommendations people get, and just describe it, the baseline, here's what it is, here's what it does, here's how people will benefit, here's what happens if they follow our advice, blah, blah, blah. Then you take those two categories, the avatar and then description of the newsletter service, and you combine them and give it, so you create a prompt where you have description of the avatar and then description of the service. And then you say, you use, and I can give you like exact language, but essentially what you're looking for is to say, analyze these two categories using metaphorical analysis, and then cross-reference, and this is where you then start to combine, let's say the boring service, a newsletter service, with outside topics that might be interesting to the avatar. So for example, if my avatar is, and I'm just gonna just for a second example, I'm gonna use this example, let's say my avatar, I know, loves old Western movies, like they're all in on, what's his name? The old guy, Clint Eastwood. Clint Eastwood, yeah. Yeah, they love Clint Eastwood, they love old Spaghetti Westerns, like that's their jam. Then I will say, cross-reference Western spaghetti movies, Clint Eastwood, the 1800, and Wild West in the US as a topic, and then I'll include other categories like popular stories or other categories that I want to put together. And then I say, give me new, unique, uncommon, non-generic, surprising, fascinating, interesting ways to use dividend investing to generate monthly income. I just wanna stop you for a second there, because that's so important, what I've learned from you, is be specific and be redundant. Don't be afraid to be redundant. Interesting, specific, non-generic, fascinating, relevant. Adjectives and synonyms are your best friends when you interact with AI, because the more specific you are, the better. And you should even say, be weird and right like a human, with incomplete sentences and odd phrasings. So you give it those instructions and you say, like the avatar wants to use, or is hoping that dividend investing will generate monthly income, just as an example. All of that together with the cross-references, with what you're looking for, and then the information about the avatar and the service, put it all into a prompt, hit enter, and it'll generate metaphors, analogies, anything you want that combines the idea of spaghetti westerns with dividend investing. And so it'll help you see or formulate dividend investing in new and interesting and compelling ways. Switch out the topic of spaghetti investing, and let's say they're all in on Frank Sinatra and Elvis Presley, and like these archetypes of human artists that are singers, songwriters, whatever, and you then say, like combine dividend investing with Frank Sinatra, Elvis Presley, and other popular singers. And what it'll do is that it'll take your boring topic, spin it into something that is linked with and expressed through the lens of Frank Sinatra. So I'm sure it'll say something like investing my way, like referencing a Sinatra song, as a way for, as a investing philosophy for you to use when you approach dividend investing. Maybe, Elvis Presley, it'll pick out an Elvis Presley song like Heartbreak Hotel, like don't have your heart broken over dividends, like, and then do this instead. So it'll combine widely different topics to help you express a common topic in new, unique, and interesting ways. And it's endless, and it can combine, like you can take Buddhism and dividend investing. You can take like how to survive in the African savannah if you find yourself lost one day and dividend investing. Any weird thing you can think of, and you should think of, interesting and odd pairings, so that through the process of working through those combinations, and you do this for 10, 15 minutes, and you give it weird other topics to combine with dividend investing, it'll express it all for you. And by doing that for 10, 15 minutes, you'll have at least four or five really, really interesting new spins and new ways to look at, talk about, and express dividend investing. One of the things I've really learned from you is, you don't just go in there and say, give me 10 really good ideas for a newsletter for dividend investing, right? You take it really, because then you'll just get, generic, you know, junk, right? It'll take, oh, I know these things that look like ideas for things, and I'll plug this into it. But when you're really specific, when you give it prompts like, give me, combine it with this, and even you're even going to the step of metaphorically, right, you're not saying ideas, because it doesn't really know what an idea is per se. Exactly, exactly. You're saying, what can be a metaphor that uses Clint Eastwood, that uses Frank Sinatra? And what could be an analogy, and also like switch up, and I do this, I ask for metaphors, I ask for analogies, I ask for scientific sounding terms, I ask for first person stories, I ask for emotional diary entries or journal entries, I ask for what would, if my avatar is in a relationship, let's say it's a husband and wife, I'll ask for like, what would a wife say to her husband? And let's say the wife is, let's say the wife is an expert on baking, and like, she has a cafe that she runs, and I'll just say that in the prompt, like the wife is an expert on baking, and she runs a cafe, what would, and I'll literally put in the prompt, what would she say to her husband about dividend investing that makes use of her expertise and knowledge of baking? Then it'll combine the topic of baking, expressed as something the wife would say to the husband, on the topic of dividend investing. So this is what I said earlier, imagine anything, put it into words and it'll do it, anything. That's amazing.
